data.text: IŌĆÖve confirmed the federation package already has the forwarding test scaffolding, so IŌĆÖm reading the existing auth middleware and hub/spoke paths now to see whether this is a small augmentation or a broader refactor.

The current forwarding helper already preserves arbitrary headers, so the main gap is likely on the hub/spoke auth side rather than the federation client. IŌĆÖm reading the existing trust gate and the forward-mutation handler next.

I have the forwarding handler and the existing trust gate. Next IŌĆÖm locating the current `requireTrusted` tests so I can preserve their localhost and ts-net behavior while adding the new forwarded-mutation coverage.

data.text: The client-side forwarding helper is simple pass-through today. IŌĆÖm checking whether the repo already has any reusable WhoIs or Tailscale identity helpers I can lean on, because that determines whether the new server-side check can stay small.
I hit a shell invocation issue while fetching a few files, so IŌĆÖm sanity-checking the worktree path and then IŌĆÖll continue with the exact file reads needed for the change.
I found the issue: the worktree path needs the `T` timestamp separator. IŌĆÖm back on the correct tree now and reading the remaining federation files before I edit.

cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3356:  """Submit an operator prompt as a proposed bead. Requires a valid CSRF token via the X-CSRF-Token request header (HTTP transport), and an idempotency key that dedupes repeated submissions within a 24-hour window. The resulting bead is in the proposed status (not auto-queued for execution); audit captures the originating identity (localhost user OR ts-net WhoIs)."""
cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3359:  """Approve a proposed operator-prompt bead, transitioning it from proposed ŌåÆ open so the execute-loop can claim it. Requires a valid CSRF token. The originating identity must be on the per-project auto-approve allowlist; ts-net identities are never permitted (locked decision). An audit event of kind operator_prompt_approve records the approver. Errors when the bead is missing, is not an operator-prompt, is not in the proposed status, or the caller is not on the allowlist."""
cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3365:  """Requeue an existing run by reopening its originating bead so the execute-loop can claim it again. Atomic with respect to the mandatory idempotencyKey: concurrent calls with the same key dedupe to the originating bead and never enqueue twice. An audit event of kind run_requeue is appended to the originating bead capturing operator identity, run id, and the optional layer override."""

After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.

## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)

- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug
- GraphQL under the nested path
- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404
- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths
- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST

## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)

### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced

B1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.

Wire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.

Create the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.

The legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header ŌĆö the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.

### 2. Audit attribution not populated

F1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.

Find every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(&request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None ŌĆö the existing `actor: String` field still works.

### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database

`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: &str = "x-axon-database"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:

(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR
(b) A new field on every request proto

(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `"{tenant}:{database}"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.

Also add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.

For this bead, the gRPC change is small:
- Rename the metadata constant
- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form
- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form
- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`

### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred ŌĆö not needed right now)

D2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior ŌĆö defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.

## Scope summary

This bead does exactly three things:

1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)
2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present
3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata

## Tests for this bead

### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)

- create a JwtIssuer with a known key
- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)
- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses
- send a request with Authorization: Bearer <jwt> to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections
- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)

### Integration test: attribution populated

- send an entity-create request with a JWT
- query the audit log
- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub

### gRPC test: composite slug

- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`
- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)

### Manual smoke test

Start the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.

## Out of scope

- DatabaseRouter replacement ŌĆö deferred
- OIDC federation ŌĆö follow-up
- Signing key rotation ŌĆö future ADR
- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs ŌĆö they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs ŌĆö install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)
- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs ŌĆö construct JwtIssuer at startup
- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs ŌĆö rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path
- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs ŌĆö NEW
- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh ŌĆö NEW
- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts ŌĆö update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs ŌĆö B1 is canonical, just consume it
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs ŌĆö legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal
- crates/axon-storage/* ŌĆö no storage changes
- crates/axon-core/* ŌĆö no type changes

.as_secs()\n+}\n+\n+// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test fixtures the pre-seeded auth storage (user + tenant member).\n+fn make_auth_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n+    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n+    storage.insert_user(User {\n+        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        display_name: \"Cutover User\".to_string(),\n+        email: None,\n+        created_at_ms: 0,\n+        suspended_at_ms: None,\n+    });\n+    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n+        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n+        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n+        role: TenantRole::Write,\n+    });\n+    storage\n+}\n+\n+/// Arc::new(TokioMutex::new(AxonHandler::new(data_storage)));\n+    let tenant_router = Arc::new(TenantRouter::single(handler.clone()));\n+\n+    let app = build_router_with_auth(\n+        tenant_router,\n+        \"memory\",\n+        None,\n+        AuthContext::no_auth(),\n+        RateLimitConfig::default(),\n+        ActorScopeGuard::default(),\n+        Some(control_plane),\n+ vec![Op::Read],\n+        }],\n+    }\n+}\n+\n+fn make_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n+    let now = now_secs();\n+    let claims = JwtClaims {\n+        iss:  let jw = issuer();\n+    let (server, _handler) = make_server(jw);\n+\n+    // No Authorization header ŌåÆ goes through no-auth path ŌåÆ 201\n+    let resp = server\n+   <bead id="axon-dccbdf73">
    <title>auth-jwt-pipeline: sign + verify with revocation cache + request extension</title>
    <description>
Implement the full JWT verification middleware in axon-server.

## What you're building

An axum middleware layer that runs on every data-plane request. It validates the JWT, installs a (user_id, tenant_id, grants) tuple into the request extension, and returns a typed AuthError on any failure. Downstream handlers read the extension and trust it completely.

## Scope ŌĆö exactly what to add

### New file: `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`

A module exposing two things:

1. A `JwtIssuer` helper that signs a `JwtClaims` into a compact JWT string. HS256 is the only algorithm required for this bead. The signing key is a `Vec<u8>` passed in at construction. Provide `issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError>` and a `verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` for round-tripping in tests.

2. An axum middleware `async fn jwt_verify_layer` that runs the 8-step verification order below. On success, installs a `ResolvedIdentity { user_id, tenant_id, grants }` into the request extension. On failure, returns a response built via `AuthError::into_response()` so the mapping to HTTP status + error.code is consistent.

The middleware also owns an `InMemoryRevocationCache` (a small `tokio::sync::RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>` is fine; nothing fancy) and falls back to SQL (via the storage adapter's new `is_jti_revoked` method) on cache miss.

### Extend: `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs`

- Add `ResolvedIdentity { pub user_id: UserId, pub tenant_id: TenantId, pub grants: Grants }`.
- Add `impl AuthError` with `pub fn status_code(&self) -> u16` returning 401 or 403 per ADR-018 ┬¦4 failure table, and `pub fn error_code(&self) -> &'static str` returning the stable code string. (An axum `IntoResponse` impl can live in axon-server.)
- Add `impl Grants` with `pub fn find_database(&self, name: &str) -> Option<&GrantedDatabase>` and `impl GrantedDatabase` with `pub fn has_op(&self, op: Op) -> bool`.

### Extend: `crates/axon-storage/src/adapter.rs` (trait)

Add one method to the `StorageAdapter` trait:

```rust
async fn is_jti_revoked(&self, jti: Uuid) -> Result<bool, AxonError>;
```

Implement it in `crates/axon-storage/src/memory.rs` (store a `HashSet<Uuid>`; default-empty), `crates/axon-storage/src/sqlite.rs`, and `crates/axon-storage/src/postgres.rs` (`SELECT 1 FROM credential_revocations WHERE jti = $1`). The A1 schema (`auth_schema.rs`) already has the `credential_revocations` table ŌĆö just query it.

### Wire into: `crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs`

Re-export `auth_pipeline::{jwt_verify_layer, JwtIssuer, InMemoryRevocationCache}`. Don't wire it into the main router yet ŌĆö that's D-phase work. For this bead, the middleware just needs to exist and be testable in isolation.

## Verification order (normative ŌĆö ADR-018 ┬¦4)

1. Extract `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` from the request. Missing header ŌåÆ `AuthError::Unauthenticated` (401). The `--no-auth` bypass is NOT part of this bead ŌĆö that's E2.
2. Parse and verify the JWT signature with HS256 against the issuer's key. Signature invalid ŌåÆ `CredentialInvalid` (401). Malformed structure ŌåÆ `CredentialMalformed` (401). `aud` as a JSON array ŌåÆ `CredentialMalformed` (this falls out of the A2 `parse_claims` wrapper ŌĆö use it).
3. Check `nbf` and `exp` against `SystemTime::now()` with a 30s skew budget. `exp` more than 30s in the past ŌåÆ `CredentialExpired` (401). `nbf` more than 30s in the future ŌåÆ `CredentialNotYetValid` (401).
4. Check `claims.jti` against the revocation cache, then SQL on miss. Hit ŌåÆ `CredentialRevoked` (401). Populate the cache on SQL hit to speed up subsequent checks.
5. Compare `claims.aud` to the `{tenant}` segment extracted from the URL path. Mismatch ŌåÆ `CredentialWrongTenant` (**403**, not 401 ŌĆö see the table). The URL extraction path is: look at `request.uri().path()` and parse the leading `/tenants/{tenant}/databases/{database}/...` segments. For this bead, implement a small helper `fn extract_tenant_database(path: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` that returns `None` for non-data-plane paths so they skip the middleware.
6. Look up the `sub` UUID in the `users` table via a new `StorageAdapter::get_user(UserId) -> Result<Option<User>, AxonError>` method. User not found or `user.suspended_at_ms.is_some()` ŌåÆ `UserSuspended` (401).
7. Check tenant membership: a new `StorageAdapter::get_tenant_member(TenantId, UserId) -> Result<Option<TenantMember>, AxonError>`. Missing ŌåÆ `NotATenantMember` (403).
8. Walk `claims.grants.databases[]` looking for an entry matching the URL `{database}`. None ŌåÆ `DatabaseNotGranted` (403). Found ŌåÆ compute the required op from the HTTP method (GET = Read, POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE = Write) and intersect with the grant's ops. Empty intersection ŌåÆ `OpNotGranted` (403). Admin-requiring routes are out of scope for this bead ŌĆö the middleware only handles Read/Write classification.
9. Build `ResolvedIdentity` and install it via `request.extensions_mut().insert(resolved)`.

## Error mapping ŌĆö must exactly match ADR-018 ┬¦4

The mapping table is copied here verbatim. Do not invent variants; the 14 AuthError variants already exist in axon-core.

| Failure | Status | `error.code` |
|---|---|---|
| No Authorization header | 401 | `unauthenticated` |
| Header not `Bearer <token>` or base64 broken | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| JWT structurally invalid / missing claims | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| `aud` as JSON array | 401 | `credential_malformed` |
| Signature invalid | 401 | `credential_invalid` |
| `exp` in past (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_expired` |
| `nbf` in future (beyond skew) | 401 | `credential_not_yet_valid` |
| `jti` revoked | 401 | `credential_revoked` |
| `iss` unknown | 401 | `credential_foreign_issuer` ŌĆö for this bead, reject if `claims.iss != issuer.issuer_id` |
| `aud` ŌēĀ URL tenant | **403** | `credential_wrong_tenant` |
| `sub` suspended/deleted | 401 | `user_suspended` |
| `sub` not a tenant member | **403** | `not_a_tenant_member` |
| URL database not in grants | **403** | `database_not_granted` |
| Op not in grant | **403** | `op_not_granted` |

Note the 401 vs 403 split: credential-level problems are 401, scope-level problems are 403.

## Testing ŌĆö all must pass for this bead to be complete

### Unit tests in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs`

For every row of the error mapping table, build the minimal defective request and assert the middleware produces exactly that `(status, error_code)` pair. Table-driven with `#[test]` or a single `#[test]` that iterates an array of cases.

Additionally:

- `jwt_roundtrip_succeeds`: issue a valid JWT with `JwtIssuer::issue`, verify it with `JwtIssuer::verify`, assert claims match.
- `valid_jwt_populates_request_extension`: run the full middleware on a valid JWT, assert the response is 200 and the handler sees `ResolvedIdentity` in extensions.
- `revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second`: issue a valid JWT, verify it succeeds, insert the jti into the revocation cache, verify the next request returns 401 `credential_revoked`. Do NOT use sleep ŌĆö invalidate the cache directly.
- `clock_skew_30s_accepted_31s_rejected`: construct a JWT with `exp` at `now - 29s` (should succeed) and one with `exp` at `now - 31s` (should fail with `credential_expired`). Do the same for `nbf`.
- `ops_matrix`: for each HTTP method in {GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE} and each op set in the grant, assert the expected 200/403 outcome.

### Integration test in `crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs`

Build a minimal axum router with one dummy route `GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping`. Install the middleware. Hit it with:
- No auth header ŌåÆ 401 `unauthenticated`
- Valid JWT with `grants: [{name: "orders", ops: [read]}]` ŌåÆ 200
- Valid JWT with the wrong tenant in aud ŌåÆ 403 `credential_wrong_tenant`
- Valid JWT with grants for a different database ŌåÆ 403 `database_not_granted`

### axon-sim workload `inv_018_grant_enforcement`

**If this is too large for one pass, skip the axon-sim workload and note it in the commit message for a follow-up bead.** The core verification must ship; the simulation workload can follow. Do not block the whole bead on the workload.

## Crate dependencies you will need

Add to `crates/axon-server/Cargo.toml` under `[dependencies]`:
```toml
jsonwebtoken = "9"
```

Everything else (axum, tower, tokio, uuid, serde_json, http) is already in the workspace.

## Context you must know about

### A1 landed: schema
- `crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs` has the SQL for `users`, `user_identities`, `tenant_users`, `credential_revocations`, etc.
- `user_identities.external_id` (NOT `subject`) ŌĆö already corrected.
- Migration runs via `apply_auth_migrations_sqlite` / `_postgres`.

### A2 landed: types
- `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` has all the types: `TenantId`, `UserId`, `User`, `UserIdentity`, `Tenant`, `TenantMember`, `TenantRole`, `Op`, `GrantedDatabase`, `Grants`, `JwtClaims`, `AuthError` (14 variants).
- `parse_claims(&str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError>` wraps serde_json and maps errors to `CredentialMalformed`. USE IT.
- `AUTH_ERROR_VARIANT_COUNT == 14` is enforced via compile-time exhaustive match.
- Delegation helper on `TenantRole`: `admin.can_delegate(op)` / etc.

### Files you should NOT touch
- Anything outside `crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs`, `crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs` (additive), `crates/axon-storage/src/{adapter,memory,sqlite,postgres}.rs` (additive), and the two new test files.
- The existing `crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs` ŌĆö that's the Tailscale whois path. Leave it alone. Your pipeline is a new module.

## Quality bar

You are the implementation step. A post-merge review with opus will verify the result against the AC. If your commit misses an AC item, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher tier. **Don't commit partial work hoping the review slides.** If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write a `no_changes_rationale.txt` explaining exactly what's done and what's blocking, and do NOT commit ŌĆö the bead will be re-queued.

Specifically: if you cannot make `cargo test -p axon-server` and `cargo clippy -p axon-server -- -D warnings` green, do not commit. Fix the issues first.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs exists and compiles
- [ ] JwtIssuer can sign and verify a JwtClaims round-trip (HS256)
- [ ] Middleware implements the 8-step verification order
- [ ] Every row of the ADR-018 ┬¦4 failure table has a matching unit test asserting (status, error_code)
- [ ] Revocation cache invalidation test passes without sleep
- [ ] Clock skew test passes at 30s boundary (both directions)
- [ ] Integration test with a dummy protected route covers the 4 golden paths
- [ ] is_jti_revoked wired into memory/sqlite/postgres storage adapters (trait + 3 impls)
- [ ] get_user and get_tenant_member wired similarly
- [ ] ResolvedIdentity lives in axon-core and is installed into the axum request extension
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-server green
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-storage green (new trait methods added)
- [ ] cargo test -p axon-core green (new helpers added)
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean

You are a coding agent implementing a single bead inside an isolated git worktree. Work from the bead description above ŌĆö it is complete and unambiguous. The AC list is the completion contract.

## How to work

1. **Read first, act second.** Read the governing ADR-018 sections and the existing crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs so you understand the AuthError variants and the parse_claims wrapper. Then read crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs to confirm the credential_revocations table shape. Then start coding.

2. **Cross-reference your work against the AC as you go.** Every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a piece of code you can point to. Before committing, re-read the AC and physically tick each one.

3. **Run the verification commands locally before committing.** cargo test and cargo clippy per the AC. If either fails, fix it. Do not commit red code.

4. **Commit once, when everything works.** Do not commit partial or exploratory changes. The bead is a single atomic unit of work.

5. **If you cannot complete the bead**, write `.ddx/executions/b1-enhanced-manual/no_changes_rationale.txt` with (a) what's done, (b) what's blocking, (c) why a follow-up bead is needed. Do not commit red code to "make progress".

## Constraints

- Work only in the files listed under "Scope" and "Files you should NOT touch" above.
- Do not modify CLAUDE.md or add new documentation.
- Do not run `ddx init`.
- Keep the `.ddx/executions/` directory intact ŌĆö DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- Commit with `git add <specific files> && git commit -m 'feat(auth-pipeline): ... [axon-dccbdf73]'`. Do not `git add -A` ŌĆö there may be other WIP in the worktree.

## Done definition

`cargo test -p axon-server -p axon-core -p axon-storage` green + `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings` clean + every AC item ticked + one commit. That's done.

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_013gJCuoGDTeTdaN2WBPFa9t","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! SQL migrations for auth/tenancy tables (FEAT-014, ADR-018).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Adds tables for:\n4\t//! - tenants: global account boundary\n5\t//! - users: global user identities\n6\t//! - user_identities: federated identity mapping\n7\t//! - tenant_users: M:N tenant membership\n8\t//! - tenant_databases: tenant-to-database mapping\n9\t//! - credential_revocations: revoked JWTs\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Note: `audit_retention_policies` is intentionally NOT included here ŌĆö it\n12\t//! belongs to F1 audit-attribution (a separate bead / feature).\n13\t\n14\t/// Apply auth/tenancy migrations to a SQLite connection.\n15\t///\n16\t/// This is idempotent ŌĆö running it multiple times on the same database\n17\t/// produces the same result without errors or duplicate rows.\n18\tpub fn apply_auth_migrations_sqlite(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> Result<(), String> {\n19\t    // Create tenants table.\n20\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n21\t    conn.execute_batch(\n22\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n23\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n24\t            name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n25\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n26\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n27\t            updated_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL\n28\t        )\",\n29\t    )\n30\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n31\t\n32\t    // Create users table.\n33\t    conn.execute_batch(\n34\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n35\t            id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n36\t            display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n37\t            email              TEXT,\n38\t            created_at_ms      INTEGER NOT NULL,\n39\t            suspended_at_ms    INTEGER\n40\t        )\",\n41\t    )\n42\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n43\t\n44\t    // Create user_identities table.\n45\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n46\t    conn.execute_batch(\n47\t        \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n48\t            provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n49\t            external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n50\t            user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n51\t            created_at_ms  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n52\t            PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n53\t        )\",\n54\t    )\n55\t    .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Create tenant_users table.\n58\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required apply_auth_migrations_postgres(client: &tokio_postgres::Client) -> Result<(), String> {\n99\t    // Create tenants table.\n100\t    // UNIQUE(name) enforced by the UNIQUE constraint on the name column.\n101\t    client\n102\t        .batch_execute(\n103\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenants (\n104\t                id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n105\t                name               TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,\n106\t                display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n107\t                created_at_ms      BIGINT NOT NULL,\n108\t                updated_at_ms      BIGINT NOT NULL\n109\t            )\",\n110\t        )\n111\t        .await\n112\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenants table: {e}\"))?;\n113\t\n114\t    // Create users table.\n115\t    client\n116\t        .batch_execute(\n117\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (\n118\t                id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n119\t                display_name       TEXT NOT NULL,\n120\t                email              TEXT,\n121\t                created_at_ms      BIGINT NOT NULL,\n122\t                suspended_at_ms    BIGINT\n123\t            )\",\n124\t        )\n125\t        .await\n126\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"users table: {e}\"))?;\n127\t\n128\t    // Create user_identities table.\n129\t    // PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id) enforces UNIQUE(provider, external_id).\n130\t    client\n131\t        .batch_execute(\n132\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_identities (\n133\t                provider       TEXT NOT NULL,\n134\t                external_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n135\t                user_id        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n136\t                created_at_ms  BIGINT NOT NULL,\n137\t                PRIMARY KEY (provider, external_id)\n138\t            )\",\n139\t        )\n140\t        .await\n141\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"user_identities table: {e}\"))?;\n142\t\n143\t    // Create tenant_users table.\n144\t    // PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id) is the composite PK required by ADR-018.\n145\t    client\n146\t        .batch_execute(\n147\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_users (\n148\t                tenant_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n149\t                user_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),\n150\t                role         TEXT NOT NULL,\n151\t                added_at_ms  BIGINT NOT NULL,\n152\t                PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, user_id)\n153\t            )\",\n154\t        )\n155\t        .await\n156\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_users table: {e}\"))?;\n157\t\n158\t    // Create tenant_databases table.\n159\t    client\n160\t        .batch_execute(\n161\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tenant_databases (\n162\t                tenant_id      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id),\n163\t                database_name  TEXT NOT NULL,\n164\t                created_at_ms  BIGINT NOT NULL,\n165\t                PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, database_name)\n166\t            )\",\n167\t        )\n168\t        .await\n169\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"tenant_databases table: {e}\"))?;\n170\t\n171\t    // Create credential_revocations table.\n172\t    client\n173\t        .batch_execute(\n174\t            \"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS credential_revocations (\n175\t                jti            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n176\t                revoked_at_ms  BIGINT NOT NULL,\n177\t                revoked_by     TEXT\n178\t            )\",\n179\t        )\n180\t        .await\n181\t        .map_err(|e| format!(\"credential_revocations table: {e}\"))?;\n182\t\n183\t    Ok(())\n184\t}\n185\t\n186\t#[cfg(test)]\n187\tmod tests {\n188\t    use super::*;\n189\t    use {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01DGpwMtL9EAnaRPBK3GqEBs","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Axon server ŌĆö HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! # Authentication\n4\t//!\n5\t//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n6\t//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n7\t//!\n8\t//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n9\t//! |------|------|-------|------|\n10\t//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n11\t//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n12\t//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n13\t//!\n14\t//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n15\t//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n16\t//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n17\t//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n18\t//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n19\t//!\n20\t//! ACL tag ŌåÆ role mapping:\n21\t//! - `tag:axon-admin` ŌåÆ [`Role::Admin`]\n22\t//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` ŌåÆ [`Role::Write`]\n23\t//! - `tag:axon-read` ŌåÆ [`Role::Read`]\n24\t//! - no matching tag ŌåÆ `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n25\t//!\n26\t//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n27\t//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n28\t//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n29\t//!\n30\t//! # Request flow\n31\t//!\n32\t//! ```text\n33\t//! TCP accept\n34\t//!   ŌööŌöĆ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n35\t//!        Ōö£ŌöĆ AuthContext::resolve_peer  ŌåÆ  Identity\n36\t//!        ŌööŌöĆ insert Identity into request extensions\n37\t//!             ŌööŌöĆ route handler\n38\t//!                  Ōö£ŌöĆ extract Extension<Identity>\n39\t//!                  Ōö£ŌöĆ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n40\t//!                  ŌööŌöĆ handler logic\n41\t//! ```\n42\t//!\n43\t//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\n44\tpub mod actor_scope;\n45\tpub mod auth;\n46\tmod collection_listing;\n47\tpub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\n48\tpub mod control_plane;\n49\tpub mod control_plane_routes;\n50\tpub mod cors_config;\n51\tpub mod user_roles;\n52\tpub mod gateway;\n53\tpub mod idempotency;\n54\tpub(crate) mod mcp_http;\n55\tpub mod mcp_stdio;\n56\tpub mod rate_limit;\n57\tpub mod schema_registry;\n58\tpub mod serve;\n59\tpub mod service;\n60\tpub mod tenant_router;\n61\t\n62\tpub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\n63\tpub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\n64\tpub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n65\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TSMSYvQzdh78YH3Sxyiy8q","session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"b2e10ba8-f883-42a2-b408-1004661a2ce3","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:30:53.127Z"}

id         TEXT NOT NULL,\n25\t///     version    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n26\t///     data       TEXT NOT NULL,\n27\t///     PRIMARY KEY (collection, id)\n28\t/// );\n29\t/// ```\n30\t///\n31\t/// Transactions use SQLite's `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` / `COMMIT` / `ROLLBACK`\n32\t/// statements. `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` acquires a write lock up-front, eliminating\n33\t/// the TOCTOU window that exists when a read and write are issued as separate\n34\t/// statements.\n35\tpub struct SqliteStorageAdapter {\n36\t    conn: Mutex<Connection>,\n37\t    /// `true` while a `BEGIN` has been issued but not yet committed or rolled back.\n38\t    in_tx: bool,\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\timpl SqliteStorageAdapter {\n42\t    /// Opens (or creates) a SQLite database at the given path.\n43\t    pub fn open(path: &str) -> Result<Self, AxonError> {\n44\t        let conn = Connection::open(path).map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n45\t        let adapter = Self {\n46\t            conn: Mutex::new(conn),\n47\t            in_tx: false,\n48\t        };\n49\t        adapter.init_schema()?;\n50\t        Ok(adapter)\n51\t    }\n52\t\n53\t    /// Opens an in-memory SQLite database (useful for testing).\n54\t    pub fn open_in_memory() -> Result<Self, AxonError> {\n55\t        let conn = Connection::open_in_memory().map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n56\t        let adapter = Self {\n57\t            conn: Mutex::new(conn),\n58\t            in_tx: false,\n59\t        };\n60\t        adapter.init_schema()?;\n61\t        Ok(adapter)\n62\t    }\n63\t\n64\t    /// Acquire the inner `Connection` lock, converting a poisoned-mutex error into\n65\t    /// an `AxonError::Storage`.\n66\t    fn conn(&self) -> Result<MutexGuard<'_, Connection>, AxonError> {\n67\t NULL PRIMARY KEY\n78\t                );\n79\t                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS namespaces (\n80\t                    database_name TEXT NOT NULL,\n81\t                    name          TEXT NOT NULL,\n82\t                    PRIMARY KEY (database_name, name),\n83\t                    FOREIGN KEY (database_name) REFERENCES databases(name) ON DELETE CASCADE\n84\t                );\n85\t Role-based and attribute-based access control for Axon (FEAT-012).\n1\t//!\n2\t//! # Layers\n3\t//!\n4\t//! **Layer 1 ŌĆö RBAC** ([`Role`], [`CallerIdentity`], [`Operation`])\n5\t//!\n6\t//! Four built-in roles (`Admin > Write > Read > None`) control access to\n7\t//! Axon operations.  Roles are derived from the identity provider (Tailscale\n8\t//! ACL tags, OIDC claims, etc.) by the server layer and passed into handlers\n9\t//! as a [`CallerIdentity`].  Handlers call [`CallerIdentity::check`] to\n10\t//! enforce the minimum required role.\n11\t//!\n12\t//! **Layer 2 ŌĆö Field-level masking** ([`MaskPolicy`])\n13\t//!\n14\t//! A `MaskPolicy` hides individual entity fields from callers whose role\n15\t//! falls below a configured minimum.  Applied by [`CallerIdentity::apply_masks`]\n16\t//! before returning entity data to the caller.  Admins always see all fields.\n17\t//!\n18\t//! **Layer 3 ŌĆö Collection write control** ([`WritePolicy`])\n19\t//!\n20\t//! A `WritePolicy` sets a per-collection minimum write role and marks fields\n21\t//! as immutable after creation.  Checked by the handler before any mutation.\n22\t//!\n23\t//! **Layer 4 ŌĆö Database-scoped grants** ([`DatabaseGrant`], [`GrantRegistry`])\n24\t//!\n25\t//! Grants scope a role to a specific database (or `\"*\"` for all databases).\n26\t//! Without a matching grant a caller has `Role::None` on that database.\n27\t//! Admins with a global grant bypass per-database checks.  The grant registry\n28\t//! is stored in `__axon_policies__` and enforced at the routing layer.\n29\t\n30\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n31\t\n32\tuse crate::error::AxonError;\n33\t\n34\t/// Built-in roles for Axon RBAC (FEAT-012).\n35\t///\n36\t/// Roles are ordered by privilege: `Admin > Write > Read > None`.\n37\t/// When a user has multiple roles (e.g., from multiple ACL tags),\n38\t/// the highest-privilege role wins.\n39\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n40\tpub enum Role {\n41\t    /// No access. Explicitly denied.\n42\t    None = 0,\n43\t    /// Read entities, query, traverse, browse audit log.\n44\t    Read = 1,\n45\t    /// Create, update, delete entities and links in any collection.\n46\t    Write = 2,\n47\t    /// All operations including drop and schema changes.\n48\t    Admin = 3,\n49\t}\n50\t\n51\timpl Role {\n52\t    /// Parse a role from a Tailscale ACL tag or string name.\n53\t    ///\n54\t    /// Accepted inputs (both full `tag:` prefix and bare name):\n55\t    ///\n56\t    /// | Input | Role |\n57\t    /// |-------|------|\n58\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-admin\"` / `\"tag:admin\"` / `\"admin\"` | `Admin` |\n59\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-write\"` / `\"tag:axon-agent\"` / `\"tag:write\"` / `\"write\"` | `Write` |\n60\t    /// | `\"tag:axon-read\"` / `\"tag:read\"` / `\"read\"` | `Read` |\n61\t    /// | anything else | `None` |\n62\t    ///\n63\t    /// `tag:axon-agent` is an alias for `write` ŌĆö the conventional tag for\n64\t    /// automated agent workloads that need read/write but not admin access.\n65\t    pub fn from_tag(tag: &str) -> Self {\n66\t        match tag {\n67\t            \"tag:axon-admin\" | \"tag:admin\" | \"admin\" => Role::Admin,\n68\t            \"tag:axon-write\" | \"tag:axon-agent\" | \"tag:write\" | \"write\" => Role::Write,\n69\t            \"tag:axon-read\" | \"tag:read\" | \"read\" => Role::Read,\n70\t            _ => Role::None,\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Determine the highest-privilege role from a set of tags.\n75\t    pub fn from_tags(tags: &[String]) -> Self {\n76\t        tags.iter()\n77\t            .map(|t| Role::from_tag(t))\n78\t            .max()\n79\t            .unwrap_or(Role::None)\n80\t    }\n81\t}\n82\t\n83\timpl std::fmt::Display for Role {\n84\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n85\t        match self {\n86\t            Role::None => write!(f, \"none\"),\n87\t            Role::Read => write!(f, \"read\"),\n88\t            Role::Write => write!(f, \"write\"),\n89\t            Role::Admin => write!(f, \"admin\"),\n90\t        }\n91\t    }\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// The kind of operation being attempted.\n95\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n96\tpub enum Operation {\n97\t    /// Read an entity, query, traverse, audit.\n98\t    Read,\n99\t    /// Create, update, delete entities/links.\n100\t    Write,\n101\t    /// Drop collections, modify schemas, manage namespaces.\n102\t    Admin,\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\timpl Operation {\n106\t    /// The minimum role required for this operation.\n107\t    pub fn required_role(self) -> Role {\n108\t        match self {\n109\t            Operation::Read => Role::Read,\n110\t            Operation::Write => Role::Write,\n111\t            Operation::Admin => Role::Admin,\n112\t        }\n113\t    }\n114\t}\n115\t\n116\t/// A simple field-equality filter used as an agent scope constraint\n117\t/// (FEAT-022 / ADR-016).\n118\t///\n119\t/// When attached to a [`CallerIdentity`], the agent guardrails layer rejects\n120\t/// any mutation whose target entity data does not satisfy\n121\t/// `data[field] == value`. V1 supports only field-equality predicates;\n122\t/// compound and range filters are deferred.\n123\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n124\tpub struct EntityFilter {\n125\t    /// The top-level data field to compare.\n126\t    pub field: String,\n127\t    /// The required value for that field.\n128\t    pub value: serde_json::Value,\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\timpl EntityFilter {\n132\t    /// Convenience constructor.\n133\t    pub fn new(field: impl Into<String>, value: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n134\t        Self {\n135\t            field: field.into(),\n136\t            value,\n137\t        }\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Returns `true` when the JSON object `data` satisfies this filter.\n141\t    ///\n142\t    /// Returns `false` when `data` is not a JSON object, when the field is\n143\t    /// absent, or when the value does not equal [`Self::value`].\n144\t    pub fn matches(&self, data: &serde_json::Value) -> bool {\n145\t        data.get(&self.field).is_some_and(|v| v == &self.value)\n146\t    }\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/// Identity context for a request.\n150\t///\n151\t/// Populated by the auth middleware from the identity provider.\n152\t/// In `--no-auth` mode, all requests get `CallerIdentity::anonymous()`.\n153\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n154\tpub struct CallerIdentity {\n155\t    /// Actor name for audit log entries.\n156\t    pub actor: String,\n157\t    /// The caller's effective role (highest privilege from all their tags).\n158\t    pub role: Role,\n159\t    /// Optional scope constraint enforced by the agent guardrails layer\n160\t    /// (FEAT-022, ADR-016). When `Some`, this caller may only mutate entities\n161\t    /// whose data matches the filter. `None` means no scope restriction.\n162\t    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n163\t    pub entity_filter: Option<EntityFilter>,\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\timpl CallerIdentity {\n167\t    /// Create an anonymous identity with admin privileges (`--no-auth` mode).\n168\t    pub fn anonymous() -> Self {\n169\t        Self {\n170\t            actor: \"anonymous\".into(),\n171\t            role: Role::Admin,\n172\t            entity_filter: None,\n173\t        }\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Create an identity from a name and role.\n177\t    pub fn new(actor: impl Into<String>, role: Role) -> Self {\n178\t        Self {\n179\t            actor: actor.into(),\n180\t            role,\n181\t            entity_filter: None,\n182\t        }\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Attach an entity scope filter (builder style, FEAT-022).\n186\t    pub fn with_entity_filter(mut self, filter: EntityFilter) -> Self {\n187\t        self.entity_filter = Some(filter);\n188\t        self\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Check whether this caller is authorized for the given operation.\n192\t    ///\n193\t    /// Returns `Ok(())` if authorized, or `Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation)`\n194\t    /// with a descriptive message if not.\n195\t    pub fn check(&self, op: Operation) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n196\t        let required = op.required_role();\n197\t        if self.role >= required {\n198\t            Ok(())\n199\t        } else {\n200\t            Err(AxonError::InvalidOperation(format!(\n201\t                \"permission denied: role '{}' cannot perform {:?} operations (requires '{}')\",\n202\t                self.role, op, required\n203\t            )))\n204\t        }\n205\t    }\n206\t\n207\t    /// Apply field-level masking to entity data based on mask policies.\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// Admin users see all fields. For other roles, fields listed in a\n210\t    /// mask policy that requires a higher role than the caller's are\n211\t    /// removed from the data.\n212\t    pub fn apply_masks(&self, data: &mut serde_json::Value, policies: &[MaskPolicy]) {\n213\t        if self.role >= Role::Admin {\n214\t            return; // Admin sees all.\n215\t        }\n216\t        if let Some(obj) = data.as_object_mut() {\n217\t            for policy in policies {\n218\t                if self.role < policy.min_role {\n219\t                    obj.remove(&policy.field);\n220\t                }\n221\t            }\n222\t        }\n223\t    }\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t/// A field-level mask policy (US-046, FEAT-012).\n227\t///\n228\t/// Specifies that a field should be hidden from callers whose role is\n229\t/// below `min_role`. Admin always sees all fields.\n230\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n231\tpub struct MaskPolicy {\n232\t    /// The field name to mask.\n233\t    pub field: String,\n234\t    /// Minimum role required to see this field.\n235\t    pub min_role: Role,\n236\t}\n237\t\n238\t// ŌöĆŌöĆ Attribute-based write control (US-047, FEAT-012) ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n239\t\n240\t/// Per-collection write policy for attribute-based access control (ABAC).\n241\t///\n242\t/// Stored in the `__axon_policies__` pseudo-collection. Defines:\n243\t/// - Minimum role required to write to this collection\n244\t/// - Fields that are immutable after creation\n245\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, .expect(\"two-part entity should exist\")\n                .data[\"scope\"],\n            json!(\"billing\")\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            s.get(&fully_qualified, &entity_id)\n Tests that use the default `current_thread` scheduler must\n    /// call adapter methods from a `spawn_blocking` closure.\n    fn block<T>(\n        &self,\n        fut: impl Future<Output = Result<T, self.block(self.client.batch_execute(\"ROLLBACK\"))?;\n        self.in_tx = false;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn create_database(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if self.database_exists(name)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\"database '{name}'\")));\n        }\n\n        self.block(\n            self.client\n                .execute(\"INSERT INTO databases (name) VALUES ($1)\", &[&name]),\n        )?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO namespaces (database_name, name) VALUES ($1, $2)\",\n            &[&name, &DEFAULT_SCHEMA],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_databases(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(\n            self.client\n                .query(\"SELECT name FROM databases ORDER BY name ASC\", &[]),\n        )?;\n        Ok(rows.iter().map(|row| row.get(\"name\")).collect())\n    }\n\n    fn drop_database(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if !self.database_exists(name)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\"database '{name}'\")));\n        }\n\n        let doomed = self.database_collection_keys(name)?;\n        self.purge_links_for_collections(&doomed)?;\n        self.block(\n            self.client\n                .execute(\"DELETE FROM entities WHERE database_name = $1\", &[&name]),\n        )?;\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM collection_views WHERE database_name = $1\",\n            &[&name],\n        ))?;\n        self.block(\n            self.client\n                .execute(\"DELETE FROM schemas WHERE database_name = $1\", &[&name]),\n        )?;\n        self.block(\n            self.client\n                .execute(\"DELETE FROM collections WHERE database_name = $1\", &[&name]),\n        )?;\n        self.block(\n            self.client\n                .execute(\"DELETE FROM databases WHERE name = $1\", &[&name]),\n        )?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn create_namespace(&mut self, namespace: &Namespace) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        if self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"DELETE FROM namespaces\n                 WHERE database_name = $1 AND name = $2\",\n            &[&namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_namespace_collections(\n        &self,\n        namespace: &Namespace,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        if !self.namespace_exists(namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\"namespace '{namespace}'\")));\n        }\n\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 WHERE database_name = $1 AND schema_name = $2\n                 ORDER BY name ASC\",\n            &[&namespace.database, &namespace.schema],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(rows\n            .iter()\n            .map(|row| {\n                let name: String = row.get(\"name\");\n                CollectionId::new(name)\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn append_audit_entry(&mut self, mut entry: AuditEntry) -> Result<AuditEntry, AxonError> {\n        if entry.timestamp_ns == 0 {\n            entry.timestamp_ns = SystemTime::now()\n                .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n                .map(|d| d.as_nanos() as u64)\n                .unwrap_or(0);\n        }\n\n        let entry_json = serde_json::to_value(&entry)?;\n\n        let row = self.block(self.client.query_one(\n            \"INSERT INTO audit_log (timestamp_ns, collection, entity_id, version, mutation, actor, transaction_id, entry_json)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8)\n                 RETURNING id\",\n            &[\n                &(entry.timestamp_ns as i64),\n u64;\n\n        Ok(entry)\n    }\n\n    fn put_schema(&mut self, schema: &CollectionSchema) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(&schema.collection)?;\n        let => Ok(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn get_schema_version(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n        version: u32,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionSchema>, AxonError> {\n        let key = self.resolve_catalog_key(collection)?;\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT schema_json FROM schemas\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND u32,\n                    row.get::<_, i64>(\"created_at_ns\") as u64,\n                )\n            })\n            .collect())\n    }\n\n    fn delete_schema(&mut self, collection: &CollectionId) AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n self.resolve_catalog_key(&view.collection)?;\n        if !self.collection_exists_in_namespace(&key.collection, &key.namespace)? {\n            return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n              \"SELECT version FROM collection_views\n                 WHERE collection = $1 AND database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n                &[\n                    &key.collection.as_str(),\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n                    &key.namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?\n            .map_or(0, |row| row.get::<_, i32>(\"version\"));\n        let next_version = current_version + 1;\n\n        let updated_at_ns = SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_nanos() as i64;\n\n        let mut versioned = view.clone();\n        versioned.collection = key.collection.clone();\n        versioned.version = next_version as u32;\n        versioned.updated_at_ns = Some(updated_at_ns as u64);\n        let view_json = serde_json::to_value(&versioned)?;\n\n        self.block(self.client.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO collection_views\n                    (collection, database_name, schema_name, version, view_json, updated_at_ns, updated_by)\n                 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7)\n                 ON CONFLICT (database_name, schema_name, collection) DO UPDATE SET\n                     version = EXCLUDED.version,\n                     view_json = EXCLUDED.view_json,\n                     updated_at_ns = EXCLUDED.updated_at_ns,\n                     updated_by = EXCLUDED.updated_by\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n                &next_version,\n                &view_json,\n                &updated_at_ns,\n                &versioned.updated_by,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        Ok(versioned)\n    }\n\n    fn get_collection_view(\n        &self,\n        collection: &CollectionId,\n    ) -> Result<Option<CollectionView>, AxonError> {\n        let key = = $2 AND schema_name = $3\",\n            &[\n                &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n\n        match rows.first() {\n            Some(row) => {\n                let view_json: serde_json::Value = row.get(\"view_json\");\n                let view: CollectionView = &key.collection.as_str(),\n                &key.namespace.database,\n                &key.namespace.schema,\n            ],\n        ))?;\n        if raw_collection != key.collection.as_str() {\n            self.block(self.client.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM schemas\n                     WHERE collection = $1\n                       AND ((database_name = $2 AND schema_name = $3)\n                            OR (database_name = $4 AND schema_name = $5))\",\n                &[\n                    &raw_collection,\n                    &key.namespace.database,\n &key.namespace.schema,\n                    &default_namespace.database,\n                    &default_namespace.schema,\n                ],\n            ))?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    fn list_collections(&self) -> Result<Vec<CollectionId>, AxonError> {\n        let rows = self.block(self.client.query(\n            \"SELECT name FROM collections\n                 ORDER BY provisioning ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// Validate a PostgreSQL database name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\n///\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    if name.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\n            \"database name must not be empty\".to_owned(),\n        ));\n    }\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\"non-empty checked above\");\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() && first != '_' {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\"\n        )));\n    }\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\n            \"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\"\n        )));\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\n/// `dbname` key.\n///\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// Both libpq keywordŌĆōvalue format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: &str, tenant_db_name: &str) -> String {\n    let target = format!(\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\");\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgres://\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\"postgresql://\") {\n        // URL format: replace path component.\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\"://\") {\n            let after_scheme = &superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\n                let scheme_authority = &superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\n                let rest = &after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\n                    &rest[q..]\n                } else {\n                    \"\"\n                };\n                return format!(\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\");\n            }\n            // No path: append the target database.\n            return format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\");\n        }\n        format!(\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\")\n    } else {\n        // KeywordŌĆōvalue format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\n        let mut parts: Vec<String> = superadmin_dsn\n            .split_whitespace()\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\"dbname=\"))\n            .map(str::to_owned)\n            .collect();\n        parts.push(format!(\"dbname={target}\"));\n        parts.join(\" \")\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\n/// superadmin connection.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\n///\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"CREATE DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\n///\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\n///\n/// # Errors\n///\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: &str, name: &str) -> Result<(), AxonError> {\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\n    let full_name = format!(\"axon_{name}\");\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\n        .enable_all()\n        .build()\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n    rt.block_on(async {\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let _ = connection.await;\n        });\n        let row = client\n            .query_one(\n                \"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\",\n                &[&full_name.as_str()],\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\n        if !exists {\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\n                \"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\"\n            )));\n        }\n        client\n            .simple_query(&format!(\"DROP DATABASE \\\"{full_name}\\\"\"))\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\n        Ok(())\n    })\n}\n\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\n// Explicit verification path:\n// AXON_TEST_POSTGRES=\"host=localhost user=axon dbname=axon_test\" cargo test -p axon-storage postgres::tests:: -- --nocapture\n// When `AXON_TEST_POSTGRES` is unset, these tests attempt to provision PostgreSQL\n// via testcontainers and skip cleanly if the container runtime is unavailable.\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::{\n        ops::{Deref, DerefMut},\n TestSetupError::Fail(AxonError::Storage(format!(\n                    \"failed to resolve PostgreSQL test container port: {error}\"\n                )))\n            })?;\n\n            Ok(Self {\n                url: format!(\n                    \"host={host} port={port} user=postgres password=postgres dbname=axon_test\"\n                ),\n                _container: Some(container),\n JWT-based auth pipeline: sign, verify, and enforce credentials (ADR-018 ┬¦4).\n//!\n//! # Verification order\n//!\n//! 1. On failure, returns a JSON error response built from `AuthError::status_code` and\n//! `AuthError::error_code`.\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex, RwLock};\nuse ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// Signs and verifies JWTs using HS256.\npub struct JwtIssuer {\n    secret: Vec<u8>,\n    /// Issuer identifier placed in the `iss` claim and verified on every request.\n    pub issuer_id: String,\n}\n\nimpl JwtIssuer {\n    /// Create a new issuer with the given HMAC secret and issuer ID.\n    pub fn new(secret: Vec<u8>, issuer_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self { secret, issuer_id }\n    }\n\n    /// Sign `claims` into a compact HS256 JWT string.\n    pub fn issue(&self, claims: &JwtClaims) -> Result<String, AuthError> {\n        encode(\n            &Header::new(Algorithm::HS256),\n            claims,\n            &EncodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret),\n        )\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)\n    }\n\n    /// Verify the signature and deserialise the claims.\n    ///\n    /// Time-based checks (`exp`, `nbf`) are intentionally skipped here; the\n    /// middleware performs them manually with a configurable skew budget.\n    pub fn verify(&self, token: &str) -> Result<JwtClaims, AuthError> {\n        let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);\n        validation.validate_exp = false;\n        validation.validate_nbf = false;\n        validation.validate_aud = false;\n        validation.required_spec_claims = HashSet::new();\n\n        decode::<JwtClaims>(token, &DecodingKey::from_secret(&self.secret), &validation)\n            .map(|td| td.claims)\n            .map_err(|e| match e.kind() {\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidSignature => AuthError::CredentialInvalid,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Json(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Base64(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::Utf8(_) => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                jsonwebtoken::errors::ErrorKind::InvalidToken => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n                _ => AuthError::CredentialMalformed,\n            })\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ InMemoryRevocationCache ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// In-process cache of revoked JWT IDs.\n///\n/// The middleware checks this first before falling back to the storage adapter,\n/// avoiding a round-trip to storage on every request for known-revoked tokens.\n#[derive(Default)]\npub struct InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    inner: RwLock<HashSet<Uuid>>,\n}\n\nimpl InMemoryRevocationCache {\n    /// Create an empty cache.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if `jti` is in the cache.\n    pub fn contains(&self, jti: &Uuid) -> bool {\n        self.inner\n            .read()\n            .map(|g| g.contains(jti))\n            .unwrap_or(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Add `jti` to the cache.\n    pub fn insert(&self, jti: Uuid) {\n        if let Ok(mut g) = self.inner.write() {\n            g.insert(jti);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ AuthPipelineState ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// Shared state for the JWT verification middleware.\n///\n/// Wrap in `Arc` before passing to `axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state`.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Token issuer / verifier.\n    pub issuer: Arc<JwtIssuer>,\n    /// Fast in-process revocation check.\n    pub revocation_cache: Arc<InMemoryRevocationCache>,\n    /// Authoritative storage for user, membership, and revocation data.\n    pub storage: Arc<Mutex<Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AuthPipelineState {\n    /// Convenience constructor.\n    pub fn new(issuer: JwtIssuer, storage: Box<dyn StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n            revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n            storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Middleware ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// Axum middleware that enforces the ADR-018 ┬¦4 JWT verification order.\n///\n/// Install with:\n/// ```ignore\n/// Router::new()\n///     .route(\"/tenants/:t/databases/:db/ŌĆ”\", ŌĆ”)\n///     .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n///         Arc::new(state),\n///         jwt_verify_layer,\n///     ))\n/// ```\npub async fn jwt_verify_layer(\n    State(state): State<Arc<AuthPipelineState>>,\n    request: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let path = request.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    // Skip middleware for paths that are not data-plane paths.\n    let Some((tenant, database)) = extract_tenant_database(&path) else {\n        return next.run(request).await;\n    };\n\n    let tenant = tenant.to_string();\n    let database = database.to_string();\n    let method = request.method().clone();\n    let headers = request.headers().clone();\n\n    match verify_request(&state, &headers, &method, &tenant, &database) {\n        Ok(identity) => {\n            let mut request = request;\n            request.extensions_mut().insert(identity);\n            next.run(request).await\n        }\n        Err(err) => AuthErrorResponse(err).into_response(),\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Core verification logic ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\nfn verify_request(\n    state: &AuthPipelineState,\n    headers: &HeaderMap,\n    method: &Method,\n    tenant: &str,\n    database: &str,\n) -> Result<ResolvedIdentity, AuthError> {\n    // Step 1: Extract Bearer token.\n    let token = extract_bearer_token(headers)?;\n\n    // Step 2: Verify JWT signature and structure.\n    let claims = state.issuer.verify(token)?;\n\n    // Step 3: Check iss.\n    if claims.iss != state.issuer.issuer_id {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialForeignIssuer);\n    }\n\n    // Step 4: Check exp/nbf with 30 s skew.\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs();\n    const SKEW: u64 = 30;\n    // Expired: exp + skew < now  Ōåö  expired more than `skew` seconds ago\n    if claims.exp.saturating_add(SKEW) < now {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialExpired);\n    }\n    // Not yet valid: nbf > now + skew  Ōåö  valid more than `skew` seconds from now\n    if claims.nbf > now.saturating_add(SKEW) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialNotYetValid);\n    }\n\n    // Step 5: Check JTI revocation (cache first, then storage).\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims\n        .jti\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|_| AuthError::CredentialMalformed)?;\n    if state.revocation_cache.contains(&jti_uuid) {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n    }\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        if storage\n            .is_jti_revoked(jti_uuid)\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?\n        {\n            // Populate the cache so subsequent checks avoid another storage round-trip.\n            drop(storage);\n            state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n            return Err(AuthError::CredentialRevoked);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 6: Compare aud to URL tenant.\n    if claims.aud != tenant {\n        return Err(AuthError::CredentialWrongTenant);\n    }\n\n    // Step 7: Look up user, check suspension.\n    let user_id = UserId::new(&claims.sub);\n    let user = {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_user(user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::UserSuspended)?\n    };\n    if user.suspended_at_ms.is_some() {\n        return Err(AuthError::UserSuspended);\n    }\n\n    // Step 8: Check tenant membership.\n    let tenant_id = TenantId::new(tenant);\n    {\n        let storage = state\n            .storage\n            .lock()\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::Unauthenticated)?;\n        storage\n            .get_tenant_member(tenant_id.clone(), user_id.clone())\n            .map_err(|_| AuthError::NotATenantMember)?\n            .ok_or(AuthError::NotATenantMember)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 9: Walk grants for the URL database + HTTP-method-derived op.\n    let granted_db = claims\n        .grants\n        .find_database(database)\n Axon server ŌĆö HTTP gateway, gRPC service, MCP stdio, and authentication.\n//!\n//! # Authentication\n//!\n//! Identity is resolved once per request by [`auth::AuthContext`] and\n//! injected as a typed extension.  Three modes are supported:\n//!\n//! | Mode | Flag | Actor | Role |\n//! |------|------|-------|------|\n//! | `NoAuth` | `--no-auth` | `\"anonymous\"` | Admin |\n//! | `Tailscale` | *(default)* | node name | from ACL tags |\n//! | `Guest` | `--guest-role <role>` | `\"guest\"` | configured role |\n//!\n//! In `Tailscale` mode the server contacts the local Tailscale daemon over its\n//! Unix socket (`/run/tailscale/tailscaled.sock` by default) and calls\n//! `/localapi/v0/whois?addr=<peer>` to resolve the connecting node's identity.\n//! Resolved identities are cached by peer IP for the duration of\n//! `--auth-cache-ttl-secs` (default 60 s).\n//!\n//! ACL tag ŌåÆ role mapping:\n//! - `tag:axon-admin` ŌåÆ [`Role::Admin`]\n//! - `tag:axon-write` / `tag:axon-agent` ŌåÆ [`Role::Write`]\n//! - `tag:axon-read` ŌåÆ [`Role::Read`]\n//! - no matching tag ŌåÆ `--tailscale-default-role` (default `read`)\n//!\n//! Connections that are not on the tailnet are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC\n//! `UNAUTHENTICATED`.  If the Tailscale daemon is unreachable the server\n//! returns HTTP 503 / gRPC `UNAVAILABLE`.\n//!\n//! # Request flow\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! TCP accept\n//!   ŌööŌöĆ authenticate_http_request  (gateway.rs middleware)\n//!        Ōö£ŌöĆ AuthContext::resolve_peer  ŌåÆ  Identity\n//!        ŌööŌöĆ insert Identity into request extensions\n//!             ŌööŌöĆ route handler\n//!                  Ōö£ŌöĆ extract Extension<Identity>\n//!                  Ōö£ŌöĆ identity.require_read() / require_write() / require_admin()\n//!                  ŌööŌöĆ handler logic\n//! ```\n//!\n//! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`].\npub mod actor_scope;\npub mod auth;\nmod collection_listing;\npub(crate) mod embedded_ui;\npub mod control_plane;\npub mod control_plane_routes;\npub mod cors_config;\npub mod user_roles;\npub mod gateway;\npub mod idempotency;\npub(crate) mod mcp_http;\npub mod mcp_stdio;\npub mod rate_limit;\npub mod schema_registry;\npub mod serve;\npub mod service;\npub mod tenant_router;\n\npub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};\npub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;\npub use service::AxonServiceImpl;\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":6,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" //! gRPC follows the same pattern via [`service::AxonServiceImpl::authorize`]."," pub mod actor_scope;"," pub mod auth;","+pub mod auth_pipeline;"," mod collection_listing;"," pub(crate) mod embedded_ui;"," pub mod control_plane;"]},{"oldStart":60,"oldLines":5,"newStart":61,"newLines":6,"lines":[" pub mod tenant_router;"," "," pub use auth::{AuthContext, AuthMode, Identity, Role};","+pub use auth_pipeline::{InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};"," pub use mcp_stdio::run_mcp_stdio;"," pub use service::AxonServiceImpl;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
