{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01CK6wwgEq2n1LP4QVdfHsPL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01G11hf2mEssXRvin3Et23Gc","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_test.rs","content":"//! Unit tests for the JWT auth pipeline (ADR-018 ┬¦4).\n//!\n//! Covers:\n//! - Every row of the failure-mode table (14 variants)\n//! - JWT round-trip\n//! - ResolvedIdentity installation in request extensions\n//! - Revocation cache invalidation\n//! - Clock-skew boundary at ┬▒30 s\n//! - HTTP-method ├Ś grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test helpers ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn get_req(path: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ JWT round-trip ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ ADR-018 ┬¦4 failure-mode table ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header ŌåÆ 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` ŌåÆ 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", \"Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz\")\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid ŌåÆ 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array ŌåÆ 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n boundary at ┬▒30 s\n//! - HTTP-method ├Ś grant-ops matrix\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\n    jwt_verify_layer,\n};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test helpers ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\nconst ISSUER_ID: &str = \"test-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"user-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"super-secret-key-for-tests\";\nconst TEST_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn valid_claims() -> JwtClaims {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\n            }],\n        },\n    }\n}\n\nfn make_storage() -> MemoryStorageAdapter {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Test User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    storage\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let storage: Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync> =\n        Box::new(make_storage());\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service call should succeed\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"body read should succeed\");\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n bearer_req(path: &str, token: &str) -> Request<Body> {\n    Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(path)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap()\n}\n\nfn error_code(json: &Value) -> &str {\n    json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\")\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ JWT round-trip ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[test]\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\");\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(&token).expect(\"verify should succeed\");\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, claims.aud);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.jti, claims.jti);\n    assert_eq!(decoded.grants, claims.grants);\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ ADR-018 ┬¦4 failure-mode table ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n/// Row 1: No Authorization header ŌåÆ 401 unauthenticated\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_no_auth_header() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = get_req(TEST_PATH);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"unauthenticated\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2a: Header present but not `Bearer` ŌåÆ 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid ŌåÆ 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, \"not.a.valid.jwt\");\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 2c: `aud` as JSON array ŌåÆ 401 credential_malformed\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_aud_is_array() {\n    #[derive(Serialize)]\n    struct ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: String,\n        sub: String,\n        aud: Vec<String>,\n        jti: String,\n        iat: u64,\n        nbf: u64,\n        exp: u64,\n        grants: Grants,\n    }\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let bad_claims = ArrayAudClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: vec![TENANT.to_string()], // array, not string\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: vec![Op::Read],\n            }],\n        },\n    };\n    let token = jsonwebtoken::encode(\n        &jsonwebtoken::Header::new(jsonwebtoken::Algorithm::HS256),\n        &bad_claims,\n        &jsonwebtoken::EncodingKey::from_secret(SECRET),\n    )\n    .unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_malformed\");\n}\n\n/// Row 3: Signature invalid ŌåÆ 401 credential_invalid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_signature_invalid() {\n    let wrong_issuer = JwtIssuer::new(b\"wrong-secret\".to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let token = wrong_issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_invalid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 4: `exp` in past beyond skew ŌåÆ 401 credential_expired\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_exp_expired() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_expired\");\n}\n\n/// Row 5: `nbf` in future beyond skew ŌåÆ 401 credential_not_yet_valid\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_nbf_future() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.nbf = now + 31; // 31 seconds from now, beyond the 30s skew\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_not_yet_valid\");\n}\n\n/// Row 6: `jti` revoked ŌåÆ 401 credential_revoked\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_jti_revoked() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), // Insert the JTI directly into the revocation cache.\n    state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"credential_revoked\");\n}\n\n/// Row 7: `iss` unknown ŌåÆ 401 credential_foreign_issuer\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_foreign_issuer() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    claims.iss = \"some-other-issuer\".to_string();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, valid_claims();\n    claims.aud = \"other-tenant\".to_string(); // URL says \"acme\"\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n \"credential_wrong_tenant\");\n}\n\n/// Row 9: `sub` suspended ŌåÆ 401 user_suspended\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_user_suspended() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: Some(1000),\n    });\n    let state = Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string())),\n )),\n    });\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"No Member\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"not_a_tenant_member\");\n}\n\n/// Row 11: URL database not in grants ŌåÆ 403 database_not_granted\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_database_not_granted() {\n    let  let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"database_not_granted\");\n}\n\n/// Row 12: Op not in grant ŌåÆ 403 op_not_granted\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_op_not_granted() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let mut claims = valid_claims();\n    // Grant only has Read; we'll POST (Write).\n    claims.grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n\n    // POST = Write, but grant only has Read.\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"POST\")\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(error_code(&json), \"op_not_granted\");\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ valid_jwt_populates_request_extension ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\nasync fn identity_handler(\n    Extension(identity): Extension<ResolvedIdentity>,\n) -> impl IntoResponse {\n    axum::Json(serde_json::json!({\"user_id\": identity.user_id.as_str()}))\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn valid_jwt_populates_request_extension() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ));\n\n    let req = bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token);\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n    assert_eq!(json[\"user_id\"].as_str().unwrap(), USER_ID);\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn revocation_takes_effect_within_one_second() {\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n    let claims = valid_claims();\n    let jti_uuid: Uuid = claims.jti.parse().unwrap();\n    let token = issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app_before = make_app(state.clone());\n    let app_after = make_app(state.clone());\n\n    // First request must succeed.\n    let (status, _) = send(app_before, state.revocation_cache.insert(jti_uuid);\n\n    // Second request must fail with credential_revoked.\n    let (status, json) = send(app_after, bearer_req(TEST_PATH, &token)).await;\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRLMXUs4TZUS5Hhv4m4p8","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01QqPQtyiL6uPht7wAo243yE","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-dccbdf73-20260415T013041-1340da9f/crates/axon-server/tests/auth_pipeline_integration_test.rs","content":"//! Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 ┬¦4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header ŌåÆ 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants ŌåÆ 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud ŌåÆ 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database ŌåÆ 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 1: No auth header ŌåÆ 401 unauthenticated ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants ŌåÆ 200 ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud ŌåÆ 403 credential_wrong_tenant ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n Integration tests for the JWT auth middleware (ADR-018 ┬¦4).\n//!\n//! Builds a minimal axum router with a single protected data-plane route:\n//!   GET /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\n//!\n//! Tests the four golden paths required by the bead:\n//! 1. No auth header ŌåÆ 401 unauthenticated\n//! 2. Valid JWT with correct grants ŌåÆ 200\n//! 3. Valid JWT with wrong tenant in aud ŌåÆ 403 credential_wrong_tenant\n//! 4. Valid JWT with grants for a different database ŌåÆ 403 database_not_granted\n\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\nuse axum::routing::get;\nuse axum::Router;\nuse http::Request;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\nuse axon_core::auth::{\n    GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, TenantId, TenantMember, TenantRole, User, UserId,\n};\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer, jwt_verify_layer};\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\n\nconst ISSUER: &str = \"integration-issuer\";\nconst TENANT: &str = \"acme\";\nconst DATABASE: &str = \"orders\";\nconst USER_ID: &str = \"u-integration-01\";\nconst SECRET: &[u8] = b\"integration-test-secret\";\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: &str = \"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\";\n\nfn now_secs() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_secs()\n}\n\nfn make_state() -> Arc<AuthPipelineState> {\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\n    storage.insert_user(User {\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        display_name: \"Integration User\".to_string(),\n        email: None,\n        created_at_ms: 0,\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\n    });\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\n    });\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\n            Box::new(storage) as Box<dyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync>\n        )),\n    })\n}\n\nfn make_app(state: Arc<AuthPipelineState>) -> Router {\n    Router::new()\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state,\n            jwt_verify_layer,\n        ))\n}\n\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request<Body>) -> (StatusCode, Value) {\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\"service should not fail\");\n    let status = resp.status();\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\n    (status, json)\n}\n\nfn bearer(token: &str) -> String {\n    format!(\"Bearer {token}\")\n}\n\nfn valid_token(issuer: &JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -> String {\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants,\n    };\n    issuer.issue(&claims).expect(\"issue should succeed\")\n}\n\nfn orders_read_grants() -> Grants {\n    Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 1: No auth header ŌåÆ 401 unauthenticated ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_no_auth_header_returns_401() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"unauthenticated\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 2: Valid JWT with read grants ŌåÆ 200 ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_valid_jwt_with_read_grant_returns_200() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, _) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 3: Wrong tenant in aud ŌåÆ 403 credential_wrong_tenant ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_tenant_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let now = now_secs();\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\n        aud: \"different-tenant\".to_string(), // URL says \"acme\"\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\n        iat: now,\n        nbf: now,\n        exp: now + 3600,\n        grants: orders_read_grants(),\n    };\n    let token = state.issuer.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"credential_wrong_tenant\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ Test 4: Grants for different database ŌåÆ 403 database_not_granted ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn integration_wrong_database_returns_403() {\n    let state = make_state();\n    let grants = Grants {\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n            name: \"invoices\".to_string(), // URL says \"orders\"\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\n        }],\n    };\n    let token = valid_token(&state.issuer, grants);\n    let app = make_app(state);\n    let req = Request::builder()\n        .method(\"GET\")\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", bearer(&token))\n        .body(Body::empty())\n        .unwrap();\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\n    assert_eq!(status.as_u16(), 403);\n    assert_eq!(\n        json[\"error\"][\"code\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\"\"),\n        \"database_not_granted\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Embedded admin UI ŌĆö assets from `ui/build` compiled into the binary.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The `UiAssets` struct embeds every file under `ui/build` at compile time.\n4\t//! The axum handler serves them at `/ui/*path` with correct `Content-Type`\n5\t//! headers, and falls back to `index.html` for any unrecognised path so that\n6\t//! SvelteKit client-side routing works correctly.\n7\t//!\n8\t//! **Build prerequisite**: `ui/build` must exist before `cargo build`.\n9\t//! Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n28\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\n29\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n30\t    let raw = uri.path();\n31\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n32\t    let asset_path = raw\n33\t        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n34\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n35\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\n36\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n37\t        \"index.html\"\n38\t    } else {\n39\t        asset_path\n40\t    };\n41\t\n42\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\n43\t}\n44\t\n45\tfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n46\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n47\t        Some(content) => {\n48\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n49\t            Response::builder()\n50\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n51\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n52\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n53\t        }\n54\t        None => {\n55\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n56\t            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n57\t                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n58\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; Run `cd ui && bun run build` (or `npm run build`) first.\n\nuse axum::body::Body;\nuse axum::http::{header, StatusCode, Uri};\nuse axum::response::{IntoResponse, Response};\nuse rust_embed::RustEmbed;\n\n/// All files under `ui/build`, embedded at compile time.\n///\n/// The `#[folder]` path is relative to the crate root (`crates/axon-server/`),\n/// so `../../ui/build` resolves to `ui/build` in the workspace root.\n#[derive(RustEmbed)]\n#[folder = \"../../ui/build\"]\nstruct UiAssets;\n\n/// Axum handler for `GET /ui` and `GET /ui/*path`.\n///\n/// Strips the `/ui` prefix, looks up the file in [`UiAssets`], and responds\n/// with the correct `Content-Type`.  Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\n/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\npub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -> Response {\n    let raw = uri.path();\n    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\n    let asset_path = raw\n        .strip_prefix(\"/ui/\")\n        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\"/ui\"))\n        .unwrap_or(raw);\n    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\n        \"index.html\"\n    } else {\n        asset_path\n    };\n\n    serve_asset(asset_path)\n}\n\nfn serve_asset(path: &str) -> Response {\n    match UiAssets::get(path) {\n        Some(content) => {\n            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\n            Response::builder()\n                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\n                .body(Body::from(content.data))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\n        }\n        None => {\n            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\n            match UiAssets::get(\"index.html\") {\n                Some(index) => Response::builder()\n                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \"text/html; charset=utf-8\")\n                    .body(Body::from(index.data))\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()),\n                None => StatusCode::NOT_FOUND.into_response(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":66,"startLine":1,"totalLines":66}}}

fn ops_matrix() {\n    use http::Method;\n\n    struct Case {\n        method: Method,\n        ops: Vec<Op>,\n        expected_status: u16,\n    }\n    let cases = vec![\n        // GET (Read) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::GET, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        // Write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) against various grant sets\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::POST, ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PUT, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::PATCH, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Write], expected_status: 200 },\n        Case { method: Method::DELETE, ops: vec![Op::Read], expected_status: 403 },\n    ];\n\n    // Use a route that accepts all HTTP methods.\n    let handler = || async { StatusCode::OK };\n\n    for case in cases {\n        let issuer_inner = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\n        let mut claims = valid_claims();\n        claims.grants = Grants {\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\n                ops: case.ops,\n            }],\n        };\n        let token = issuer_inner.issue(&claims).unwrap();\n\n        let state = make_state();\n        // Router that handles any method on TEST_PATH.\n        let app = Router::new()\n            .route(\n                TEST_PATH,\n                axum::routing::any(handler),\n            )\n            .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n                state,\n                jwt_verify_layer,\n            ));\n\n        let req = Request::builder()\n            .method(case.method.clone())\n            .uri(TEST_PATH)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {token}\"))\n            .body(Body::empty())\n            .unwrap();\n        let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            resp.status().as_u16(),\n            case.expected_status,\n            \"method={} expected {}\",\n            case.method,\n            case.expected_status\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n// ŌöĆŌöĆ error_code / status_code consistency ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ\n\n#[test]\nfn auth_error_status_and_code_coverage() {\n    // Verify every variant {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\n\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\n\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\n\n## When to Use\n\n- After implementing a tracked issue\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\n\n## What It Does\n\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\n2. **Format** commit message:\n   - First line: `<issue-id> <concise summary>` (under 72 chars)\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\n\n## Commit Message Format\n\n```\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\n\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\n\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\n```\n\n## Examples\n\n```bash\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\n```\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2","uuid":"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31","timestamp":"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z","isSynthetic":true}

data.text: I found an adjacent ADR that uses the same ŌĆ£preserve originating identity unchangedŌĆØ idea. IŌĆÖm reading the smallest relevant section now so I can mirror the existing pattern instead of inventing a new auth shape.

data.output:
- **Backlinks.** Every bead created/changed and every artifact mutated by
  an operator-prompt execution gets an `origin_operator_prompt_id` event
  so provenance survives later edits, rebases, and history rewrites.

## Multi-node delegation policy

Per ADR-007 (federation topology), prompts run on the client node that owns
the project's queue, not on the coordinator. The submission flow:

1. The operator's browser submits to whichever node it is connected to.
2. If that node owns the project's queue, it persists the bead locally and
   wakes the local execute-loop.
3. If the receiving node is a coordinator that does not own the queue, it
   forwards the submission to the owning client node, **carrying the
   originating trust attestation unchanged** (peer identity, request ID,
   prompt SHA-256, CSRF result).
4. The owning node verifies the originating identity against its own
   `web.operator_prompt.allow_identities` allowlist before persisting the
   bead.

The trust model is uniform across read and write: "localhost OR ts-net
`WhoIs` identity." Any future coordinator-relay path MUST NOT lose the
originating attestation. Coordinator-relay without attestation forwarding
is a regression of this ADR.

## Prompt-injection threat model

Prompts arriving via `operatorPromptSubmit` are untrusted *content* from a
trusted *peer*. The threats and mitigations:

- **Hostile prompt content (injection, jailbreak, prompt smuggling).**

argue its way around it.

This scope is deliberately narrow:

- The agent already has rich capabilities for a reason ŌĆö restricting them
  for prompt-driven beads would make the feature less useful than the CLI
  (`ddx try`, `ddx work`).
- The trust boundary is the *peer identity at submission time*, not the
  *capability set at execution time*. Tightening capabilities below what
  the same peer could do via CLI would be theatre.
- The bead-vs-CLI symmetry keeps reasoning simple: "if you can run it as
  a bead from the terminal, you can run it as a prompt from the web."

## Approval flow

Default ON for ts-net peers, OFF (auto-approve) for localhost.

- Submitted beads land in `proposed` status.
- The UI shows them with an "Approve & queue" button that transitions to
  `ready`.
- `web.operator_prompt.auto_approve` flips to skip-for-trusted-localhost.
- Auto-approve fires only for configured localhost identities, NEVER for
  ts-net peers by default. A ts-net peer must be explicitly listed in both
  the per-project allowlist and an auto-approve allowlist to skip review.

## Latency mitigation

Type:     task

Parent:   ddx-1d85c927

Desc:     PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client ŌĆö any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.
- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):
- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.
- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

- ddx-468d800f ŌĆö S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.

- ddx-ba31fec8 ŌĆö S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.
- ddx-ea1ada47 ŌĆö S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.
- Performance optimization of federation routing.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).

ID:       ddx-eb75a32d
Title:    S15-7b: CoordinatorŌåÆclient mutation routing envelope (foundation)
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, story:15, kind:multi-node, area:federation
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-256af8b5
Deps:     ddx-256af8b5
Desc:     PROBLEM
Federation today supports query fan-out only (cli/internal/federation/fanout.go). For the multi-node operator-prompt path, the coordinator (hub) must route a mutation to a single spoke that owns the project, NOT fan out. No mutation routing envelope, no project-based spoke selection, and no forwarding client exist.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: FanOut function sends read queries to all spokes; has no concept of routing to a single owning spoke for mutations.
- FederationRegistry (from ddx-90f48012): stores spoke records by node_id but no lookup-by-project-id helper exists.
- No HTTP client method ForwardMutation exists in the federation package.
- Plan-locked decision (point 6 from story-15 planning): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations; mutation envelope is HTTP POST to spoke /graphql with X-DDx-Origin-Identity and X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity headers preserved verbatim.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry FederationRegistry, projectID string) (*SpokeRecord, error): looks up the spoke that owns the project; returns error when no owner registered.
- Add federation client method ForwardMutation(ctx context.Context, target *SpokeRecord, body []byte, headers map[string]string) (*http.Response, error): issues HTTP POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
- Add hub HTTP handler that accepts forwarding requests and routes by project (the handler that S15-7c and S15-7d build on top of).
- Identity header values are NOT populated in this bead (that is S15-7c / ddx-ba31fec8); this bead lays the envelope and routing.

NON-SCOPE
- Dual authorization on the spoke side (ddx-ba31fec8).
- Identity attestation structure (ddx-e2c217d1).
- Changing existing query fan-out behavior.
Accept:   1. RouteMutationToProjectOwner(registry, projectID) returns correct SpokeRecord or error when no owner registered.
2. ForwardMutation(ctx, target, body, headers) issues POST to <spoke>/graphql with supplied headers preserved verbatim.
3. Hub handler that accepts mutation forwarding requests exists and routes by project.
4. TestRouteMutation_CorrectSpoke verifies routing to the correct spoke.
5. TestRouteMutation_NoOwner_ReturnsError verifies error when project owner unknown.
6. TestForwardMutation_HeadersReachSpoke verifies headers arrive at spoke unchanged.
7. No behavior change to existing query fan-out tests.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

ID:       ddx-e2c217d1
Title:    operator-prompts: multi-node trust attestation forwarding (gates non-localhost write)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, story:15, area:server, kind:feature, blocks-remote
Parent:   ddx-1d85c927
Deps:     ddx-f171a715, ddx-0793ac75
Desc:     PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement in coordinator topology. When the hub forwards a mutation to a spoke, there is no signed attestation of origin identity, and the spoke cannot distinguish a legitimate coordinator-forwarded request from a hub-originated forgery. Without dual authorization on both origin and coordinator identity, any compromised hub can submit mutations on behalf of any operator.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: no attestation metadata structure defined; no signing of identity claims.
- cli/internal/server/server.go: requireTrusted checks only immediate transport peer (hub); does not validate origin identity independently.
- wake-on-approve routing (the 'wake-on-approve targets owning client node's loop' requirement) has no implementation path in the current federation layer.
- ddx-f171a715 (federation foundation, a dep) and ddx-0793ac75 (federated GraphQL resolvers, a dep) must land first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define attestation metadata structure: signed structured metadata carrying origin identity (WhoIs peer at coordinator edge) and coordinator identity (hub's own attested ID). Signing mechanism: HMAC or similar per plan-locked decisions.
- Spoke validates BOTH: origin identity (verified against tailnet WhoIs / loopback policy) AND hub identity (verified against registered federation peers).
- wake-on-approve: when spoke receives an approve mutation, it routes wake signal to the owning client node's execute-loop (project-ownership authoritative on the spoke).

NON-SCOPE
- Transport-level encryption (handled by tailnet).
- Key management or certificate infrastructure beyond HMAC shared secret.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior.
Accept:   1. Attestation metadata structure defined (signed) in cli/internal/federation/ or cli/internal/server/.
2. Spoke validates both origin AND hub identity on forwarded mutations; returns 403 when either fails.
3. wake-on-approve mutation routed to owning spoke's execute-loop.
4. TestAttestation_ValidOriginAndHub_Accepts verifies happy path.
5. TestAttestation_SpoofedOrigin_Rejects verifies spoofed origin identity returns 403.
6. TestAttestation_UntrustedHub_Rejects verifies untrusted hub returns 403.
7. TestWakeOnApprove_RoutesToOwningSpoke verifies wake signal reaches correct spoke.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
